_publications._publicationTitle: Optical coherence tomography of the macular ganglion cell layer in children with neurofibromatosis type 1 is a useful tool in the assessment for optic pathway gliomas.

_contextSnippet: Unlike VA, GC-IPL is not affected by age in young children, rendering GC-IPL measurements more reliable in the follow-up of OPG
resourceName: Cirrus HD-OCT device (Cirrus; Carl Zeiss Meditec, Dublin, CA, USA)
details: Unlike VA, GC-IPL is not affected by age in young children, rendering GC-IPL measurements more reliable in the follow-up of OPG
